headline: Here are three political story lines to watch this week: Texas face-off, Trump’s health and the Iran talks. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics newsletter:
EU negotiators reached a provisional deal to remove some tariffs on U.S. imports as part of the bloc’s trade deal signed last summer, ahead of a U.S. deadline to ramp up tariffs on cars
Texas Sen. John Cornyn’s primary runoff against firebrand state Attorney General Ken Paxton this week is being closely watched as a barometer of the Republican Party’s future in Texas and the country
President Trump told his new Fed chair, Kevin Warsh, to ignore the political pressure during his swearing-in ceremony—and then the president laid out what he’d like to see
Spencer Pratt, the former reality TV star of “The Hills,” is running a biting, social-media-savvy campaign to be the next mayor of Los Angeles
Possible serial killer is latest horror to upend Mexico World Cup host region — two weeks before first match
White House Tells Congo’s Soccer Team to Isolate, Citing Ebola Outbreak
Israel says new head of Hamas' military wing killed in Gaza City strikes

War with Iran has halted a hard-won improvement in housing affordability. The problem is rising mortgage rates on the back of worries that the energy crisis will stoke inflation. Read more in today's Markets A.M. newsletter:
Record-setting outside money pouring into California governor's race
